meta.title: Testimonials Carousel
meta.description: A carousel displaying testimonial posts with accent-colored cards, video modal support, and dynamic content from the testimonial post type.

tsx: { PanelBody, ToggleControl, RangeControl, BaseControl, __experimentalToggleGroupControl as ToggleGroupControl, __experimentalToggleGroupControlOption as ToggleGroupControlOption } from '@wordpress/components'

requiredPatterns.usage: For isSelected to control Swiper loop/autoplay in editor
In ThemeHeading onChange for list view naming
SSR preview of PHP-rendered carousel cards
Swiper init on SSR-rendered content (not useRefEffect)
Program taxonomy filter for automatic mode

componentStructure.darkModePattern: dark bg-transparent! with before:bg-charcoal pseudo-element (from CardsCarousel)

qualityChecklist: TSX and PHP heading/layout structure match
No hardcoded colors -- uses theme tokens and accent-color system
Swiper config identical in TSX and JS files
Video modal accessible with keyboard (Escape to close, focus trap)
CSS mask fade-out on both sides when not at initial position

attributes.name: heading
attributes.description: Main section heading (e.g., 'Not Convinced? Hear What Unpaid Interns Have to Say')

attributes.name: buttons
attributes.description: CTA buttons in the heading area

attributes.name: postsSource
attributes.description: Content selection mode: automatic (query) or manual (hand-picked)

attributes.name: selectedPosts
attributes.description: Array of testimonial post IDs for manual mode

attributes.name: postsLimit
attributes.description: Max testimonials to display in automatic mode

attributes.name: hideIfEmpty
attributes.description: Hide entire section if no testimonials found

attributes.name: selectedPrograms
attributes.description: Filter by program taxonomy in automatic mode

section.notes: Always dark mode. Uses transparent bg with charcoal pseudo-element from CardsCarousel pattern.

container.classes: container relative before:absolute before:bg-charcoal before:rounded-3xl before:-z-1 before:-inset-(--side-gutter) md:before:-inset-8
container.notes: Same as CardsCarousel -- charcoal rounded card background.

themeHeading.notes: Figma shows heading H4 (48px) at headingSize 2 and description on the right. Matches CardsCarousel/DynamicContentCarousel pattern.

swiperParent.classes: swiper-parent overflow-hidden grid grid-cols-1 grid-rows-1 relative

controlsRow.notes: Contains progress bar pagination and navigation arrows. Same as CardsCarousel.

pagination.classes: swiper-pagination flex-1 relative! h-1 bg-charcoal/20 dark:bg-white/20 rounded-full overflow-hidden
pagination.notes: Progress bar with gradient fill. Same as CardsCarousel.

navigation.notes: Arrow buttons container. Same as CardsCarousel.

navButton.notes: Uses shared .carousel-nav-btn styles from swiper.css. Gradient hover animation.

swiperElement.notes: Main Swiper container. Uses unique class 'testimonials-carousel-swiper' for JS targeting.

swiperSlide.notes: Wrapper around each card template part.

fadeMaskLeft.classes: absolute top-0 bottom-0 left-0 w-[120px] z-10 pointer-events-none
fadeMaskLeft.notes: Left fade-out gradient. Only visible when carousel is NOT at the beginning. Use CSS mask-image with linear-gradient or a div with bg gradient from charcoal to transparent. Controlled via Swiper events (slideChange) toggling a data attribute or class.
fadeMaskLeft.implementation: Use a dark charcoal gradient overlay div. Show/hide via JS on Swiper 'slideChange' event. Hidden at initial state (isBeginning), shown when scrolled past start.

fadeMaskRight.classes: absolute top-0 bottom-0 right-0 w-[120px] z-10 pointer-events-none
fadeMaskRight.notes: Right fade-out gradient. Always visible unless carousel is at the end. Same mechanism as left fade.
fadeMaskRight.implementation: Use a dark charcoal gradient overlay div. Hidden when at end (isEnd), shown otherwise.

cardOuter.notes: Outer card wrapper in card-testimonial.php. Wraps the accent-colored card.

cardInner.classes: flex flex-col h-full bg-accent rounded-3xl p-6
cardInner.notes: Main card with accent color background (bg-accent). Rounded-3xl matches Figma 25px radius. Padding 24px matches Figma margin/margin-2.

cardContentArea.notes: Content area inside card. Gap 24px (gap-6), bottom padding 32px (pb-8). flex-1 to push button to bottom.

cardName.notes: Student name. Anton font 40px (text-header-5 = 1.5rem mobile, 2xl desktop maps to font/size/3xl=40px). Leading 1.1. Text color charcoal.

cardProgram.notes: Program label. Anton font 24px (font/size/xl = header-7/header-6). Leading 1.15. Text color charcoal.

cardMessage.notes: Testimonial message. Roboto Medium Italic 16px. Leading 1.5. Text color charcoal (#24231e ~ charcoal).

cardButton.classes: flex items-center gap-2 text-charcoal font-medium text-body uppercase mt-auto cursor-pointer group
cardButton.notes: WATCH THE VIDEO button. Roboto Medium 16px, uppercase. Only shown if testimonial has a video. Opens video in modal. The arrow icon is rotated 90deg (pointing right-down like a play indicator).

cardButtonArrowIcon.notes: Arrow icon next to button text. Uses the standard ArrowRight SVG rotated 90deg or a custom play-like arrow. Per Figma, it's a chevron rotated 90deg.

videoModal.classes: fixed inset-0 z-[9999] flex items-center justify-center bg-black/80 backdrop-blur-sm
videoModal.notes: Full-screen modal overlay. No Figma design provided -- implementing a polished, accessible modal. Uses <dialog> element or div with role='dialog'. Dark backdrop with blur.
videoModal.implementation: Accessible modal with: focus trap, Escape key to close, click-outside to close, aria-modal=true, body scroll lock. Animate in with scale+opacity transition.

videoModalContent.classes: relative w-full max-w-4xl mx-4 aspect-video rounded-2xl overflow-hidden bg-charcoal shadow-2xl
videoModalContent.notes: Video container inside modal. Max width ~900px. 16:9 aspect ratio. Rounded corners.

videoModalClose.classes: absolute -top-12 right-0 text-white hover:text-accent cursor-pointer transition-colors
videoModalClose.notes: Close button positioned above the video. X icon. White with accent hover.

mobile.behavior: 1 slide per view. Cards stack naturally. Pagination/navigation row above carousel. Fade masks narrower (60px). Modal is full-width with small margin.

tablet.behavior: 2 slides per view. Fade masks at 90px width.

desktop.behavior: 3 slides per view (matching Figma). Fade masks at 120px width. Full layout as designed.

emptyState.field: hideIfEmpty attribute controls section visibility when no testimonials found

editorBehavior.swiperInit: useEffect + useState pattern (not useRefEffect) because Swiper is inside SSR content

fadeMasks.description: CSS fade-out gradients on left and right sides of carousel. Left fade only visible when carousel has scrolled past initial position. Right fade visible unless carousel is at the end. Implementation: Swiper 'slideChange'/'reachBeginning'/'reachEnd' events toggle data attributes on the swiper-parent element. CSS uses these data attributes to show/hide fade divs.
fadeMasks.initialState: Left fade hidden, right fade visible (carousel starts at beginning)
fadeMasks.endState: Left fade visible, right fade hidden
fadeMasks.implementation: Use data-at-beginning and data-at-end attributes on .swiper-parent. Fade divs use opacity transitions based on these attributes. CSS approach: [data-at-beginning='true'] .fade-left { opacity: 0 }, [data-at-end='true'] .fade-right { opacity: 0 }.

videoModal.description: When a testimonial has a video (videoSource + videoFile/videoId), the card shows a 'WATCH THE VIDEO' button. Clicking opens a fullscreen modal with the video. Supports file (HTML5 video), YouTube (iframe embed), and Vimeo (iframe embed).
videoModal.trigger: Click on 'Watch the Video' button on card

videoTypes.rendering: YouTube iframe embed with autoplay on open
Vimeo iframe embed with autoplay on open

accessibility: Focus trapped inside modal while open
Body scroll locked while modal open
Focus returns to trigger button on close
videoModal.animations: Modal fades in with backdrop. Video container scales from 95% to 100% opacity. Close animates in reverse.

card.notes: Each card is an article element for semantic grouping

decisions.decision: Use same CardsCarousel layout pattern: dark mode section with charcoal pseudo-element background, progress bar pagination + nav arrows above carousel
decisions.evidence: Spec states 'Its layout is going to be the same as the cards carousel block: blocks/CardsCarousel'

decisions.decision: 3 slides per view on desktop, 2 on tablet, 1 on mobile. spaceBetween: 24px (matching CardsCarousel, not 16px from Figma gap)
decisions.evidence: CardsCarousel uses spaceBetween: 24 with breakpoints 570:2, 900:3. Figma shows 16px gap between cards but CardsCarousel override is the reference pattern.

decisions.decision: Card background uses bg-accent (accent color system). Each testimonial post should have an accent color meta or inherit from context. The green/purple/etc in Figma is the accent color.
decisions.evidence: Spec: 'In all of the post cards, the green/purple/etc is the accent color.' Figma shows #B2EAE7 (a teal/mint) but the spec clarifies these map to the accent color system.

decisions.decision: Fade masks on sides use div overlays with charcoal gradient, controlled by Swiper events. Left fade hidden at beginning, right fade hidden at end, both visible when scrolled in middle.
decisions.evidence: Spec: 'The fade out on the sides of the carousel should show on both sides if the carousel is not on its initial state. Use mask to make those fade outs.' Figma shows right fade gradient at node I785:34805;634:40810 with linear-gradient from charcoal to transparent.

decisions.decision: Video modal opens when clicking 'Watch the Video' button on cards that have video meta. Supports file/youtube/vimeo. No Figma design -- implementing polished accessible modal.
decisions.evidence: Spec: 'If the post type has a video, it should open on a modal. The video can be from a file, youtube, or vimeo. There is no design for the modal, so make me proud.'

decisions.decision: Testimonial post type needs additional video meta fields: videoSource (string, default 'file'), videoFile (integer), videoId (string). Same pattern as team_member post type.
decisions.evidence: Spec requires video support. team_member post type already has this exact pattern (videoSource/videoFile/videoId). Testimonial currently only has name and message meta.

decisions.decision: This block is testimonial-only (not multi-post-type like DynamicContentCarousel). No postsType attribute needed. Hardcoded to query 'testimonial' post type.
decisions.evidence: Spec: 'this block will be only for the testimonial post type'

decisions.decision: Card name: text-header-5 (Anton, 24px mobile / ~28px+ desktop). Card program: font-heading text-header-6 (Anton, ~20px/xl). Card message: italic font-medium text-body (Roboto Medium Italic 16px). Card button: font-medium text-body uppercase (Roboto Medium 16px).
decisions.evidence: Figma variables: Name=font/size/3xl (40px in Figma, maps to text-header-5 which goes up to 2xl on desktop). Program=font/size/xl (24px, maps to text-header-7/header-6). Message=Roboto Medium Italic 16px. Button=Roboto Medium 16px uppercase.

decisions.decision: Card border radius is rounded-3xl (24px, close to Figma's 25px -- within tolerance). Card padding is p-6 (24px, matches Figma margin/margin-2=24px).
decisions.evidence: Figma node 634:40747 shows rounded-[25px] and p-[var(--margin/margin-2, 24px)]. Tailwind rounded-3xl = 24px, 1px diff is acceptable.

decisions.decision: Card content area has gap-6 (24px, matches Figma gap/gap-4) and bottom padding pb-8 (32px, matches Figma margin/margin-3).
Section outer padding: px-8 (32px, Figma margin/margin-3) and py-16 (64px, Figma margin/margin-5). The inner content area has py-24 (96px, Figma margin/margin-7).
decisions.evidence: Figma node I785:34805;634:40724: px-[32px] py-[var(--margin/margin-7, 96px)]. The section itself has py-[var(--margin/margin-5, 64px)].

decisions.decision: Block only supports 'automatic' and 'manual' selection modes (no 'related' mode). Filter by 'program' taxonomy in automatic mode.
decisions.evidence: Testimonial post type has 'program' taxonomy. No related mode requested in spec.

decisions.decision: All text on cards uses text-charcoal since cards have light accent-color backgrounds.
decisions.evidence: Figma shows all card text as colour-primary/primary/color-brand-charcoal (#1f1f1d).

decisions.decision: Card template part (parts/card-testimonial.php) must be updated to show the new design with name, program (from taxonomy), message, and video button. The existing card-testimonial.php is a simple blockquote+cite layout that needs to be replaced.
decisions.evidence: Figma card design differs significantly from existing card-testimonial.php. New design has name (h3), program info, styled message, and video button.

decisions.decision: Fade mask width: 120px on desktop, scaled proportionally on smaller screens via responsive classes (e.g., w-[60px] sm:w-[90px] lg:w-[120px]).
decisions.evidence: Figma fade gradient node I785:34805;634:40810 is 216px wide, but that's the full design width. Proportionally ~120px at the content boundary gives a good fade effect.

componentModifications.description: Add video meta fields (videoSource, videoFile, videoId) to the testimonial post type registration, following the same pattern as team_member post type.
Replace existing simple testimonial card with new design: accent-colored card with name, program taxonomy, styled italic message, and conditional video button.
componentModifications.breaking: Yes - existing Archive block using card-testimonial.php will also get the new design. This is intentional since the card is shared.

variations.name: Default - Automatic mode
variations.description: Automatic mode with defaults. Shows all testimonials (up to 9) with pagination and navigation.

attributes.heading: Not Convinced? Hear What Unpaid Interns Have to Say
attributes.description: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit.

variations.name: With eyebrow and buttons
variations.description: Full heading area with eyebrow, heading, description, and button.

attributes.heading: What Students Are Saying
attributes.description: Hear directly from students about their experiences.

variations.name: Manual mode
variations.description: Manual mode with hand-picked testimonials. Need to select specific testimonial post IDs.
variations.notes: Developer should populate selectedPosts with actual testimonial IDs from the database.

variations.name: Minimal - heading only
variations.description: Only heading set, all optional fields empty.

variations.name: With program filter
variations.description: Automatic mode filtered by a specific program taxonomy.
variations.notes: Developer should populate selectedPrograms with actual program term IDs.

testPage.notes: Ensure at least 4+ testimonial posts exist in the database with video meta filled (mix of file, youtube, vimeo) to test video modal functionality. Also ensure testimonials have program taxonomy terms assigned and varied content lengths.

unresolvedDecisions.question: The Figma shows a specific teal/mint color (#B2EAE7) for cards, but the spec says green/purple/etc is the accent color. Should each testimonial post have its own accent_color meta field (like pages/posts), or should cards inherit the page's accent color?
options: Each testimonial gets its own accent_color meta (per-post accent color on each card)
All cards inherit the page's accent color (uniform color across cards)
Cycle through a predefined set of accent colors (e.g., alternating)
unresolvedDecisions.defaultIfNotAsked: All cards use bg-accent (inherit page accent color, uniform). This matches the spec statement and is simplest.
unresolvedDecisions.reason: Figma shows same color on all cards. Spec says 'the green/purple/etc is the accent color' which implies using the accent color system. The simplest approach is inheriting from the page.

unresolvedDecisions.question: The card shows 'Program: Hotel and Restaurant Operation Management'. Should this come from the 'program' taxonomy term name, or from a separate meta field?
unresolvedDecisions.defaultIfNotAsked: From program taxonomy terms. The taxonomy already exists and is attached to testimonials.
unresolvedDecisions.reason: The 'program' taxonomy is already registered for testimonials. Using taxonomy terms is the standard WordPress pattern and doesn't require additional meta fields.

questionsForUser.question: Should each testimonial card have its own accent color, or should all cards use the page's accent color?
questionsForUser.defaultIfNotAsked: All cards inherit page accent color (bg-accent)
